extra.reasoning: The property requires NOT(rec_drug_a AND rec_drug_b) over all inputs. rec_drug_a is set true only by rule rec_a_from_conjunction, which fires when hypertension AND prior_gi_bleed AND seizure_history AND anticoagulated all hold. rec_drug_b is set true by rule rec_b_from_trigger whenever hypertension holds. Each of these outputs has exactly one rule, so conflict-resolution priorities and order are irrelevant to them (priorities only matter among competing rules for the same output). Crucially, the precondition for rec_drug_a includes hypertension, which is also the sole trigger for rec_drug_b. Therefore any input that activates rec_drug_a inevitably also activates rec_drug_b. With hypertension=prior_gi_bleed=seizure_history=anticoagulated=true: rec_a_from_conjunction fires (rec_drug_a=true) and rec_b_from_trigger fires (rec_drug_b=true), so both are jointly true and the mutual-exclusion property is violated. heart_failure and peptic_ulcer only influence rec_drug_c, which is irrelevant to this property, and are set false. Defaults do not rescue the property since both rules fire and override the default false.
